trade.summary: Giappone-Russia bilateral trade collapsed following Russia's full-scale invasion of Ucraina in February 2022. Giappone imposed comprehensive sanctions, and most Giapponeese companies withdrew from Russia. Giappone's stake in the Sakhalin-1 and Sakhalin-2 LNG projects became politically untenable; Giappone retained a reduced position in Sakhalin-2 for energy security reasons but exited Sakhalin-1. Trade is now a fraction of pre-war levels.
top_products_a_to_b: limited dual-use industrial goods (restricted by sanctions)
disputes: Giappone joined Western sanctions regime post-Ucraina invasion (2022)
Russia threatened to cancel Sakhalin-2 agreements

diplomatic.status: Hostile — severed normal relations post-Ukraine invasion
diplomatic.summary: Giappone-Russia relations are at a post-WWII nadir. The unresolved Northern Territories/Kuril Islands dispute — which prevented a formal peace treaty for 80 years — became irrelevant as the broader relationship collapsed after 2022. Russia expelled Giapponeese diplomats in tit-for-tat measures. Giappone designated Russia an 'unfriendly country.' Giappone co-sponsored UN resolutions condemning Russian aggression and participated in G7 sanctions coordination. Russia suspended peace treaty talks.
recent_incidents: Russia threatens Sakhalin-2 LNG concession cancellation

military.summary: Giappone has significantly increased defense spending partly in response to Russia's Ucraina war demonstrating conventional warfare risks. Russia's military cooperation with Cina and North Corea del Sud — including suspected munitions transfers to Russia by North Corea del Sud — directly affects Giappone's security environment. Russian military aircraft and naval vessels continue provocative transits near Giapponeese airspace and waters.
incidents: Russian military aircraft violations of Japanese ADIZ (ongoing)
Russian naval vessels transiting between Pacific and Arctic (increased frequency)
Russia-DPRK military cooperation affecting Japan's threat environment

financial.summary: Giapponeese firms have largely divested from Russia. Major exits include Toyota (closed St. Petersburg plant), Mitsui and Mitsubishi (reduced Sakhalin stakes), and most major Giapponeese banks cutting Russian correspondent relationships. Limited residual positions remain in energy projects for energy security reasons.

energy.summary: Giappone retains a stake in Sakhalin-2 LNG for energy security — approximately 8-9% of Giappone's LNG imports came from Sakhalin before 2022. Giappone has been actively diversifying away from Russian energy. The retained Sakhalin-2 position is politically sensitive and subject to ongoing review. Sakhalin-1 stake was surrendered after Russia forced reorganization.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has largely frozen. Giappone suspended most exchange programs and bilateral research projects. Some residual collaboration on Arctic science and seismology persists at a very low level. Academic contacts have been severed in most fields.
